home.title: What is my IP? | meuip.me
home.description: Find your public IP address, location, ISP and network details in real time. Fast, accurate and free.

whatIsMyIp.title: What is my IP?
whatIsMyIp.subtitle: Your public IP address is the unique identifier assigned to your internet connection by your ISP.

whatIsIp.heading: What is an IP address?
whatIsIp.p: An IP address (Internet Protocol address) is a numerical label assigned to each device connected to a computer network. It serves two main functions: host or network interface identification and location addressing.

ipv4vsIpv6.heading: IPv4 vs IPv6
ipv4vsIpv6.p: IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses (e.g. 203.0.113.1), while IPv6 uses 128-bit addresses (e.g. 2001:db8::1). IPv6 was created to address the exhaustion of IPv4 addresses.

whyChange.heading: Why does my IP change?
whyChange.p: Most residential ISPs assign dynamic IPs that change periodically. Static IPs are fixed and usually require a business plan.

dnsLeak.title: DNS Leak Test
dnsLeak.subtitle: Verify that your DNS queries are not leaking outside your VPN or secure connection.

explainer.heading: What is a DNS leak?
explainer.p: A DNS leak occurs when your DNS queries are sent outside your encrypted tunnel (VPN), exposing your browsing activity to your ISP or third parties.

meta.title: DNS Leak Test — Check if your VPN is leaking DNS | meuip.me
meta.description: Run a free DNS leak test to verify your VPN is protecting your DNS queries. Detect exposed DNS servers in seconds.

ipv6Test.title: IPv6 Test
ipv6Test.subtitle: Check if your connection supports IPv6 and see your IPv6 address.

explainer.heading: What is IPv6?
explainer.p1: IPv6 (Internet Protocol version 6) is the latest version of the Internet Protocol, designed to address the exhaustion of IPv4 addresses. It uses 128-bit addresses, allowing for 340 undecillion unique addresses.
explainer.p2: Most modern networks support dual-stack (both IPv4 and IPv6). If your ISP hasn't enabled IPv6, you'll only see your IPv4 address above.

meta.title: IPv6 Test — Check your IPv6 connectivity | meuip.me
meta.description: Test if your internet connection supports IPv6. Find your IPv4 and IPv6 addresses instantly.

webrtc.title: WebRTC Leak Test
webrtc.subtitle: Detect if WebRTC is exposing your real IP address, even when using a VPN.

leaked.description: Your real IP may be exposed.

explainer.heading: What is a WebRTC leak?
explainer.p1: WebRTC is a browser feature for real-time communication. It can expose your local and public IP addresses to websites without your knowledge, even when you're using a VPN.
explainer.p2: To prevent WebRTC leaks, you can disable WebRTC in your browser settings or use a browser extension.

meta.title: WebRTC Leak Test — Check if WebRTC exposes your real IP | meuip.me
meta.description: Detect WebRTC IP leaks in your browser. Check if your real IP address is being exposed to websites even while using a VPN.

whatIsIp.heading: What is an IP address?
whatIsIp.p1: An IP address (Internet Protocol address) is a unique numerical identifier assigned to every device connected to a network. It works like a postal address for the internet, telling routers where to send and receive data packets.
whatIsIp.p2: There are two types: a private IP used within your local network (like your home Wi-Fi), and a public IP — the address the rest of the internet sees. This tool detects and displays your public IP in real time.

ipv4vsIpv6.heading: IPv4 vs IPv6 — what is the difference?
ipv4vsIpv6.p1: IPv4 uses 32-bit addresses in the familiar "203.0.113.1" format, supporting around 4 billion unique addresses. Because that pool is nearly exhausted, IPv6 was introduced with 128-bit addresses (e.g. "2001:db8::1"), enabling a virtually unlimited number of connected devices.
ipv4vsIpv6.p2: Most modern ISPs provide dual-stack connectivity, meaning you have both an IPv4 and an IPv6 address. Use our IPv6 test tool to check whether your connection fully supports the newer protocol.

whyKnow.heading: Why should you know your IP address?
whyKnow.p1: Knowing your public IP address has many practical uses:
whyKnow.li1: Set up remote access (VPN, SSH, IP cameras) with your source IP
whyKnow.li2: Verify that your VPN is active and actually masking your real IP
whyKnow.li3: Diagnose connectivity issues when contacting your ISP's support
whyKnow.li4: Understand the geographic location websites and services perceive for you

vpn.heading: VPN, privacy and online anonymity
vpn.p1: A VPN (Virtual Private Network) replaces your public IP with the VPN server's IP address, hiding your real location from websites and online services. When connected to a VPN, this site should display the VPN server's IP rather than your home IP.
vpn.p2: However, beware: beyond the IP itself, other vectors can still expose your identity — such as DNS and WebRTC leaks. Use our DNS Leak Test and WebRTC Leak Test tools to make sure your VPN is protecting you end-to-end.
